evidence: If you like British WWII life stories on film, you'll like this mystery just fine.
One of the classics. If you like the old British films, you'll love this one.
There's a basis of truth to the story, but the fluff is all fiction.
OK movie, pretty standard early 90's stuff.
If bazookas and blood are your thing, then look elsewhere. This film is comprised of acting and storytelling.
Wonderful value for the dollar, and the quality of the presentations is above average.
Excellent piece of film-making. Some of the shots (particularly towards the end) are ahead of their time.
I would love to see this treated to a DVD or Blu-ray release.
Maybe Criterion is interested in a release?
Wonderful value for the dollar, and the quality of the presentations is above average.
Some of the shots (particularly towards the end) are ahead of their time.
I hope it gets a full order.
This is the one! Full length episodes (not cut down)...

summary: The user shows a strong preference for British WWII life stories and mysteries, appreciating quality production and emotional engagement. They favor slower-paced narratives that focus on storytelling and character development, while demonstrating a high sensitivity to production quality.
